executive_summary_zh: 今天只有 1 篇高信号文章，但它非常值得关注：Anthropic 量化了 agentic coding eval 中“基础设施噪声”对成绩的影响，证明环境配置本身就能带来超过模型榜单差距的分数波动。
对负责模型选型、评测平台或内部 coding benchmark 的工程负责人来说，这篇文章的核心价值在于：它把“评测环境即评测的一部分”从经验判断变成了可量化、可复现的工程问题。
executive_summary_en: There is only one strong item today, but it is highly relevant: Anthropic quantifies how infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals can swing scores by more than the gap between top models.

For engineering leaders responsible for model selection, eval platforms, or internal coding benchmarks, the key takeaway is that runtime configuration is not incidental noise but part of the measured capability.

items.title: Quantifying infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals
items.summary_zh: Anthropic 指出，在 SWE-bench、Terminal-Bench 这类 agentic coding benchmark 中，运行时基础设施并不是中立容器，而是直接影响模型能否完成任务的组成部分。其内部实验显示，仅 CPU、RAM、时间预算与资源约束方式的不同，就能让 Terminal-Bench 2.0 分数出现 6 个百分点的显著波动，甚至高于很多模型排行榜之间的差距。
items.summary_en: Anthropic argues that in agentic coding benchmarks such as SWE-bench and Terminal-Bench, runtime infrastructure is not a neutral container but part of the task itself. Their internal experiments show that differences in CPU, RAM, time budgets, and resource enforcement can produce a statistically significant 6-point swing on Terminal-Bench 2.0—larger than many leaderboard gaps between models.
items.why_it_matters_zh: 这篇文章对后端、平台和评测基础设施负责人尤其重要，因为它挑战了一个常见前提：把 benchmark 分数当作模型能力的精确代理。如果环境资源、调度方式和超时策略没有被严格标准化，那么团队实际上比较的可能是“模型 × harness × infra 配置”的组合，而不是模型本身。
items.why_it_matters_en: This matters because it challenges a common assumption: benchmark scores are often treated as precise proxies for model capability. If resource allocation, scheduling behavior, and timeout policies are not tightly standardized, teams may actually be comparing a 'model × harness × infrastructure configuration' bundle rather than the model itself.
key_takeaways_zh: Agentic coding eval 与静态 benchmark 不同，运行环境会参与问题求解过程，因此环境差异会系统性影响结果。
即便 benchmark 文档给出了推荐资源，若执行层未统一强制或强制方式不同，最终测到的能力维度也会发生变化。
在做模型采购、内部对比或回归评测时，需要把 infra 配置、资源预算和错误率作为一等元数据纳入结果解释。
key_takeaways_en: Unlike static benchmarks, agentic coding evals incorporate the runtime environment into the problem-solving loop, so environment differences can systematically alter outcomes.
Even when a benchmark specifies recommended resources, inconsistent enforcement can change what capability is actually being measured.
For model selection, internal comparisons, and regression tracking, infra configuration, resource budgets, and infra error rates should be treated as first-class evaluation metadata.

top_items: Quantifying infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals
new_terms_zh: 基础设施噪声：由 CPU、内存、超时、调度与容器限制等环境因素带来的评测分数波动。
资源约束执行方式：不仅是声明资源预算，更是如何在实际 harness / cluster 中强制这些限制。
new_terms_en: Infrastructure noise: score variance caused by environmental factors such as CPU, memory, timeouts, scheduling, and container limits.
Resource enforcement methodology: not just declaring resource budgets, but how those limits are actually imposed in the harness or cluster.
themes_zh: 评测环境正在成为 agent 能力测量的一部分，而非背景条件。
从模型榜单转向“模型 + harness + infra”整体系统比较。
themes_en: The evaluation environment is becoming part of the measured agent capability rather than a background condition.
Benchmarking is shifting from pure model comparison to comparison of the full 'model + harness + infrastructure' system.
implications_zh: 企业内部若复现不了公开 benchmark，不应第一时间归因于模型质量，先排查资源配置、超时策略与基础设施错误率。
未来更可信的 coding eval 需要发布标准化执行配置、infra 观测指标和可复现实验脚本，而不只是最终分数。
implications_en: If an enterprise cannot reproduce a public benchmark, the first suspicion should not be model quality alone; it should also inspect resource allocation, timeout policy, and infra error rates.
More trustworthy coding evals will likely need standardized execution configs, infra observability metrics, and reproducible harness scripts—not just final scores.
reading_order: Quantifying infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals
what_to_ignore_zh: 不要把单次 leaderboard 名次变化直接解读为模型真实能力差异，尤其当差距只有几个百分点时。
不要忽视 benchmark harness 与集群执行细节；这类工程实现细节可能比提示词微调更影响结果。
what_to_ignore_en: Do not interpret small leaderboard differences as pure model capability differences, especially when gaps are only a few percentage points.
Do not ignore benchmark harness and cluster execution details; these implementation choices may matter more than prompt tweaks.
